question_text: A hydraulic system uses two pistons of different sizes. The small piston has an area of 2 m² and the large piston has an area of 8 m². If a force of 100 N is applied to the small piston, what is the force produced on the large piston? (Assume the pressure is equal throughout the fluid.)
explanation: In a hydraulic system, pressure is the same throughout the fluid. First, calculate the pressure on the small piston: P = F/A = 100/2 = 50 Pa. Then use P = F/A to find the force on the large piston: 50 = F/8, so F = 400 N. The larger piston produces a larger force because it has a bigger area.
